diagrams.title: State Transition Diagram for DP
diagrams.description: A directed acyclic graph (DAG) showing how DP states connect through transitions. Each node represents a subproblem state, and edges show dependencies. This helps visualize optimal substructure and the order of computation.
diagrams.purpose: Explains how optimal solutions build from subproblems and the dependency order for bottom-up computation

diagrams.title: DP Table Computation Order
diagrams.description: A 2D grid representing a classic DP table (like for edit distance or LCS). Shows the filling order with arrows indicating which cells contribute to current cell computation. Includes annotations for base cases and final solution cell.
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrates bottom-up DP table filling and how subproblem solutions combine to form the final answer

diagrams.title: Space Optimization: Rolling Array
diagrams.description: Side-by-side comparison showing full 2D DP table vs space-optimized 1D rolling array. Demonstrates how only previous row/column values are needed, reducing space complexity from O(n×m) to O(n) or O(m).
elements: dashed lines showing which old values can be discarded
diagrams.purpose: Explains space optimization techniques in DP, showing how to reduce memory usage while maintaining correct computation
